title: Bunnie Xo Reveals She’s Been Accepted Into College Amid Jelly Roll Divorce
summary: The podcaster also teased on her podcast that she's crushing on someone new.
body: Bunnie Xo is going back to school! At least, that’s what it looks like from the podcaster’s recent Instagram Story, which shows her acceptance into a state university following her split from Jelly Roll . Related Bunnie Xo Jokingly Compares Jelly Roll Divorce to Being Released From a 10-Year Prison Sentence A Timeline of Jelly Roll & Bunnie Xo’s Relationship Jelly Roll Files for Divorce From Bunnie Xo After Nearly 10 Years of Marriage A few weeks after Bunnie and the country star’s divorce went public, the Dumb Blonde host shared a photo of her acceptance email from Arizona State University on Friday (July 3), according to multiple outlets. “Dr. Xo, loading?” she reportedly captioned the post, which was captured in screenshots. It’s unclear what Bunnie might want to study, but it’s obvious that she’s entering a brand new era now that she’s single. After the news came out in June that Jelly had filed to end their marriage of nearly 10 years in May, the influencer explained on Dumb Blonde that while she felt blindsided by his decision — which was apparently prompted by an argument in which Bunnie had angrily da…

title: Good Morning, Earth!
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams took this photo of an orbital sunrise from the International Space Station on June 26, 2026. In 24 hours, the space station makes 16 orbits of Earth, traveling through 16 sunrises and sunsets. Learn more about the orbiting laboratory. Image credit: NASA/Chris Williams
body: A bright orange sunburst illuminates Earth’s atmosphere during an orbital sunrise in this photograph from the International Space Station as it orbited 264 miles above the Caucasus Mountains. NASA/Chris Williams NASA astronaut Chris Williams took this photo of an orbital sunrise from the International Space Station on June 26, 2026. In 24 hours, the space station makes 16 orbits of Earth, traveling through 16 sunrises and sunsets. Learn more about the orbiting laboratory. Image credit: NASA/Chris Williams
